title: Critique of Judgment
description: Critique of Judgment — knowledge fragments for AI agents

memes.label: About This Wiki
memes.description: A knowledge wiki for "判断力批判(上下册)" — this page is auto-generated from book metadata

memes.label: 'Table of Contents'
memes.description: 'Table of Contents'

memes.label: Adherent Beauty
memes.description: 'Beauty that presupposes a concept of what the object ought to be, and consequently a concept of its perfection — ascribed to objects with a definite purpose.'

memes.label: Aesthetic Idea
memes.description: 'A representation of the imagination that occasions much thought without any definite concept being adequate to it; the counterpart of a rational Idea in the domain of art.'

memes.label: Agreeable
memes.description: 'That which pleases the senses in sensation — the merely subjective gratification that, unlike beauty, makes no claim to universal validity'

memes.label: Analogy
memes.description: 'A mode of indirect presentation by which Reason thinks the supersensible through a comparison with sensible relations — central to the account of the symbol and teleological judgment in Kant'

memes.label: Antinomy of Judgment
memes.description: "The unavoidable dialectic that arises when reflective judgment applies conflicting maxims — mechanism vs. teleology — both of which appear necessary for the explanation of nature"

memes.label: Antinomy of Taste
memes.description: 'The apparent contradiction between the thesis (judgment of taste is not based on concepts) and antithesis (judgment of taste is based on concepts) resolved by Kant through the distinction between determinate and indeterminate concepts'

memes.label: "Archetype of Taste"
memes.description: 'The highest model of taste — a mere Idea that each must produce in himself, which cannot be represented by concepts but only in an individual presentation; better called the Ideal of the beautiful, attainable only in the human figure.'

memes.label: Architectonic
memes.description: "The art of systems — reason's demand that all knowledge be organized into a systematic whole under a single principle, applied in the third Critique to the methodology of teleological judgment"

memes.label: Baruch Spinoza
memes.description: "Dutch rationalist philosopher (1632–1677) whose pantheistic identification of God with Nature is critiqued by Kant as an inadequate account of natural purposiveness"

memes.label: Beauty
memes.description: "The object of a pure judgment of taste — that which pleases universally without a concept, through the mere form of purposiveness without purpose"

memes.label: Causality
memes.description: 'The relation of cause and effect — in the third Critique, Kant distinguishes mechanical causality (nexus effectivus) from teleological causality (nexus finalis), grounding the distinction between determinant and reflective judgment'

memes.label: Charm
memes.description: 'Sensory gratification that may accompany but must not determine a pure aesthetic judgment of taste'

memes.label: Cognition
memes.description: 'The faculty and activity of acquiring knowledge through concepts — distinguished from aesthetic judgment, which gives no cognition of its object.'

memes.label: Universal Communicability
memes.description: 'The capacity of an aesthetic judgment to be universally shared, a key condition of the judgment of taste in Kant''s "Critique of Judgment"'

memes.label: Contingency
memes.description: 'The property of natural forms and laws being not logically necessary — a key concept in Kant''s argument that organized beings require teleological judgment, and a foundational element of the antinomy of reflective judgment.'

memes.label: Culture
memes.description: 'The cultivation of the mental powers through aesthetic experience and fine art — the ultimate purpose of nature in relation to the human species and the preparatory ground for moral disposition in Kant'

memes.label: David Hume
memes.description: "Scottish empiricist philosopher (1711–1776) whose analysis of taste and skepticism about teleology profoundly influenced Kant's critical project"

memes.label: Deduction of Taste
memes.description: "Kant's argument in §§30–40 of the third Critique that legitimates the claim of pure judgments of taste to universal subjective validity, despite being based on feeling rather than concepts"
aliases: Deduction of Pure Judgments of Taste

memes.label: Argument from Design
memes.description: 'Kant''s critical analysis of the argument from design (physico-theology) in the Critique of Teleological Judgment — a natural and persuasive but ultimately insufficient proof of a supreme intelligent cause.'

memes.label: Determinant Judgment
memes.description: "The mode of judgment that subsumes particulars under given universals — the faculty of applying rules, which proceeds from the universal to the particular"

memes.label: Dialectic of Aesthetic Judgment
memes.description: 'The resolution of the antinomy of the principles of taste — the conflict between the thesis that judgments of taste are not based on concepts and the antithesis that they are, resolved by the distinction between determinate and indeterminate concepts'

memes.label: "Discipline of Taste"
memes.description: 'The regulative and restrictive function of taste over Genius — taste disciplines Genius by clipping its wings, bringing order and clarity, and ensuring that artistic products remain purposive and universally communicable rather than lapsing into lawless freedom.'

memes.label: Disinterestedness
memes.description: 'The first moment of the judgment of taste — aesthetic pleasure is entirely disinterested, involving no concern for the real existence of the object'

memes.label: Dynamical Sublime
memes.description: 'The mode of the sublime concerned with might and dominion, in which nature is judged as fearful yet without actual fear, revealing a faculty of resistance within the mind that is superior to nature and grounded in moral freedom'

memes.label: "Editor's Introduction"
memes.description: 'Editor''s Introduction from Critique of Judgment by Immanuel Kant.'

memes.label: Edmund Burke
memes.description: "Anglo-Irish statesman and philosopher (1729–1797) whose empirical analysis of the sublime in A Philosophical Enquiry (1757) directly influenced Kant's treatment"

memes.label: Emotion
memes.description: 'Affective feeling-response that — like charm — must be excluded from the determining ground of a pure judgment of taste'

memes.label: Enthusiasm
memes.description: 'An affective state involving the Idea of the good combined with strong feeling, which Kant distinguishes from fanaticism and analyzes as an aesthetic presentation of moral ideas'

memes.label: Epicurus
memes.description: "Ancient Greek philosopher (341–270 BCE) whose atomistic materialism serves as Kant's paradigm of a system that denies all purposiveness in nature"

memes.label: Feeling of Life
memes.description: 'The subjective sensation through which the mind is conscious of the furtherance or hindrance of its vital powers, serving as the basis for aesthetic judgments of taste in Kant''s Critique of Judgment.'

memes.label: Final Cause
memes.description: 'A mode of causal connection in which the effect is conceived as the ground of its cause — the form of explanation proper to organisms and natural purposes in Kant'

memes.label: Final Purpose
memes.description: "The ultimate purpose of nature as a whole — the human being as moral subject, the sole being capable of being an end in itself within the natural order"

memes.label: Fine Art
memes.description: 'Kant''s concept of artistic production distinguished from nature, science, and craft; governed by the interplay of genius and taste.'

memes.label: Form of Purposiveness
memes.description: "The mere form of purposiveness in the representation of an object — the defining characteristic of the beautiful and the basis of aesthetic pleasure in Kant's third Critique"

memes.label: Form of the Object
memes.description: "The spatial and temporal configuration of an object insofar as it is apprehended in aesthetic reflection without a determinate concept; the proper object of a pure judgment of taste"

memes.label: "Formative Power"
memes.description: 'In Kant''s reception of Blumenbach, the faculty of matter in an organized body that produces and maintains purposive form — distinguished from mere mechanical formative power and essential to the epigenetic account of organism generation.'

memes.label: Francis Hutcheson
memes.description: "Irish philosopher (1694–1746) who originated the concept of disinterestedness in aesthetic judgment and the theory of an inner moral sense"

memes.label: Free Beauty
memes.description: 'Beauty that presupposes no concept of what the object ought to be — pure, self-subsistent, and judged solely by form.'

memes.label: Free Play of Faculties
memes.description: 'The cognitive dynamic at the heart of aesthetic judgment — the harmonious free play of imagination and understanding in response to beautiful form, without determination by a concept'

memes.label: Freedom
memes.description: 'The Idea of freedom as a bridge concept in the Third Critique — the supersensible faculty through which Reason legislates for nature and makes moral purposiveness thinkable.'

memes.label: Genius
memes.description: 'The innate mental talent through which nature gives the rule to art — the faculty of presenting aesthetic ideas that set the cognitive faculties into a free play richer than any determinate concept allows'

memes.label: Glossary of Philosophical Terms
memes.description: Glossary of Philosophical Terms from Critique of Judgment by Immanuel Kant.

memes.label: Harmony
memes.description: 'The felt accordance of imagination and understanding in their free play — the subjective ground of the pleasure in aesthetic judgment and the principle by which taste claims universal validity'

memes.label: Heautonomy
memes.description: 'The mode of legislation proper to reflective judgment — giving a law not to nature (autonomy) but to itself for its own reflection, grounding the principle of purposiveness as a subjective maxim'

memes.label: Heteronomy of Taste
memes.description: 'The subjection of taste to external grounds — empirical or rational — which Kant argues destroys the autonomy essential to pure aesthetic judgment'

memes.label: "Highest Good"
memes.description: 'The unconditioned final purpose of creation — the union of virtue and happiness in proportion to morality, which only man as noumenon can set as the ultimate end for which the world exists.'

memes.label: Ideal of Beauty
memes.description: 'The archetype of taste — an individual presentation adequate to the Idea of Reason concerning a maximum of beauty, attainable only in the human figure.'

memes.label: Imagination
memes.description: 'The faculty of presentation (Darstellung) that apprehends the manifold of intuition — in aesthetic judgment it enters free play with the understanding, and in genius it produces aesthetic Ideas that surpass determinate concepts'

memes.label: Immanuel Kant
memes.description: 'German philosopher (1724–1804), author of the three Critiques and founder of transcendental idealism; his "Critique of Judgment" bridges the theoretical and practical domains.'

memes.label: Interest
memes.description: 'The satisfaction connected with the existence of an object — explicitly excluded from the pure judgment of taste, which must be disinterested'

memes.label: Internal Purposiveness
memes.description: "The purposiveness of a thing considered in itself, according to which its parts exist for the sake of the whole, as contrasted with external utility for something else"

memes.label: Introduction
memes.description: Introduction from Critique of Judgment by Immanuel Kant.

memes.label: Isaac Newton
memes.description: 'English natural philosopher (1643–1727) whose mathematical physics served Kant as the paradigm of mechanistic explanation and the limit case for teleological judgment.'

memes.label: Manifold
memes.description: 'The aggregate of sensory data given in intuition before the Understanding unifies it under concepts — in the third Critique, the harmony of Imagination and Understanding in gathering the manifold is the basis of aesthetic pleasure'

memes.label: Mathematical Sublime
memes.description: 'The mode of the sublime concerned with magnitude and the estimation of size, in which the Imagination fails to comprehend the absolutely great and thereby reveals a supersensible faculty of mind surpassing every standard of Sense'

memes.label: Mechanical Causality
memes.description: 'The mode of causal explanation in which natural phenomena are accounted for by the laws of moving matter alone, contrasted in Kant''s Critique of Judgment with teleological causality in the explanation of organisms and natural purposes.'

memes.label: Mechanism in Nature
memes.description: "The explanatory mode by which natural phenomena are understood through their parts and efficient causes — the constitutive principle of natural science and the antithesis to teleological judgment"

memes.label: Modality
memes.description: 'The Fourth Moment of the judgment of taste — the claim that the beautiful carries a necessary relation to satisfaction grounded in a common sense'

memes.label: Moral Feeling
memes.description: 'The subjective susceptibility to moral Ideas that Kant posits as a presupposition of the judgement on the Sublime and as a bridge between aesthetic and practical reason.'

memes.label: Moral Law
memes.description: 'The objective principle of practical reason that commands unconditionally and serves as the ground of the moral feeling and the final purpose of creation.'

memes.label: Moral Proof
memes.description: 'The moral proof of the existence of God in Kant''s "Critique of Judgment," which argues from the necessity of a moral author of the world as a postulate of practical reason'

memes.label: Moral Teleology
memes.description: 'Kant''s argument that the purposiveness of nature, combined with our moral vocation, gives practical grounds for belief in a moral author of the world.'

memes.label: Natural Purpose
memes.description: "A product of nature in which every part exists for the sake of the whole and is reciprocally cause and effect — the defining object of teleological judgment in Kant's Critique"

memes.label: Necessity
memes.description: 'The Fourth Moment of the judgment of taste in Kant''s "Critique of Judgment": the exemplary necessity of aesthetic assent, grounded in the idea of a common sense'

memes.label: Noumenon
memes.description: 'The supersensible, intelligible substrate of appearances that serves as the ground of nature and freedom in Kant''s critical philosophy, central to the resolution of the antinomy of teleological judgment.'

memes.label: Objective Purposiveness
memes.description: 'The suitability of a thing for a determinate end, requiring a concept of what the thing ought to be — the basis of teleological judgment in nature'

memes.label: Organism
memes.description: 'A natural being that is both cause and effect of itself, serving as the paradigm case for teleological judgment and the concept of natural purpose.'

memes.label: Perfection
memes.description: 'Objective internal purposiveness — the agreement of a manifold with its concept of what the thing ought to be, which Kant rigorously distinguishes from beauty.'

memes.label: Pleasure
memes.description: "The feeling of the promotion of life — the subjective consciousness of the harmony of the cognitive faculties, which constitutes the determining ground of aesthetic judgment"

memes.label: Postulate of Taste
memes.description: "The presupposition that a pure judgment of taste speaks with a universal voice, entitling it to demand the agreement of all subjects without being grounded in a determinate concept"

memes.label: Preface
memes.description: Preface from Critique of Judgment by Immanuel Kant.

memes.label: Presentation
memes.description: 'Darstellung or presentation in Kant''s "Critique of Judgment": the act of making a concept sensible through intuition, central to aesthetic and symbolic cognition'

memes.label: Purposiveness
memes.description: 'The a priori principle of reflective judgment — the assumption that nature displays adaptation to ends, forming a well-ordered whole comprehensible by our cognitive faculties'

memes.label: Reason
memes.description: 'The highest cognitive faculty in Kant''s critical system — Reason supplies Ideas that unify the cognitions of Understanding and legislates the moral law for the faculty of desire.'

memes.label: Reflective Judgment
memes.description: "The faculty that seeks universals for given particulars — the mode of judgment for which purposiveness serves as the a priori principle, and the central faculty of Kant's Third Critique"

memes.label: Regulative Principle
memes.description: 'An Idea of Reason that guides inquiry without constituting the object itself — contrasted with constitutive principles that determine objects of experience'

memes.label: Relative Purposiveness
memes.description: 'A relation in which one natural thing serves as a means to the end of another — distinguished from inner purposiveness, where a thing is both cause and effect of itself, as in organisms'

memes.label: Satisfaction (Wohlgefallen)
memes.description: 'The genus of pleasure or liking that takes three distinct forms — the pleasant (gratification of inclination), the beautiful (disinterested favour), and the good (esteem through Reason) — according to Kants Analytic of the Beautiful'

memes.label: Schematism
memes.description: 'The procedure of the Judgment by which an intuition is supplied to a concept, functioning in Kant''s Critique of Judgment both objectively in determinative judgment and subjectively in the free play of imagination and understanding.'

memes.label: Sensus Communis
memes.description: "The subjective principle of taste that grounds the necessity and universal communicability of aesthetic judgments — a common sense shared by all judging subjects"

memes.label: Spirit
memes.description: 'In Kant''s aesthetic theory, spirit (Geist) is the animating principle of the mind — the faculty of presenting aesthetic Ideas that gives life to works of fine art and distinguishes genius from mere imitation.'

memes.label: Subjective Condition
memes.description: "A condition residing in the subject — rather than in the object — that makes a certain kind of judgment or cognition possible; central to Kant's account of aesthetic judgment"

memes.label: Subjective Purposiveness
memes.description: 'The accordance of the form of an object with the cognitive faculties without any determinate concept — the ground of aesthetic pleasure and the judgment of taste'

memes.label: Subjective Universality
memes.description: 'The distinctive kind of universality claimed by judgments of taste in Kant''s aesthetics — universally valid for all judging subjects without being grounded in any concept of the object.'

memes.label: Supersensible Substrate
memes.description: "The indeterminate, non-empirical ground underlying both nature and freedom — the common root that resolves the antinomy of judgment and bridges the theoretical and practical domains"

memes.label: Symbol
memes.description: 'Mode of indirect presentation in which an intuition corresponds to a concept by analogy rather than directly — most famously in Kant''s claim that the Beautiful is the symbol of the morally Good'

memes.label: Taste
memes.description: 'The faculty of judging the beautiful — a subjective yet universally valid capacity grounded in the free play of cognitive faculties'

memes.label: Teleological Judgment
memes.description: 'The mode of reflective judgment that conceives of nature in terms of ends and purposes, forming the subject of the second half of the Critique of Judgment.'

memes.label: Teleological System
memes.description: 'A systematic ordering of nature according to final causes, in which every part is both cause and effect of the whole — a regulative Idea rather than a constitutive assertion about nature'

memes.label: The Good
memes.description: 'That which pleases through the mere concept by means of Reason, always bound up with an interest in the existence of its object, and distinguished from both the pleasant and the beautiful in Kants Analytic of the Beautiful'

memes.label: The Sublime
memes.description: "Kant's analysis of the feeling that exceeds the beautiful — a negative pleasure arising when the imagination fails to comprehend what reason demands, revealing the supersensible vocation of the subject"

memes.label: Transition (Transition)
memes.description: 'The passage or bridge between two domains — centrally, the transition from the realm of nature to the realm of freedom that the Critique of Judgment makes possible'

memes.label: Understanding
memes.description: 'The faculty of concepts and rules in Kant''s critical system — one of the three higher cognitive faculties alongside Reason and Judgment, with its own legislative domain in the theoretical cognition of nature.'

memes.label: Universality
memes.description: 'The Second Moment of the judgment of taste in Kant''s "Critique of Judgment": the claim that the beautiful pleases universally without a concept'
